style: An aged color photograph in the style of a sun-faded vintage print from the late 1880s American frontier. Photographic, not illustrated — it looks like a real photograph that has slowly faded in sunlight over a century. COLOR BEHAVIOR: warm tones survive rich — burnt orange, amber, ochre, golden yellow, sienna brown, rust, wheat, tobacco. Cool tones faded and washed out — blues and greens pale, desaturated, drifting toward soft gray-sepia. Warm amber cast, gentle bloom, fine film grain, slightly low contrast, soft edges, faint vignette. MOOD: grief deferred by busyness, a house full of people with empty chairs in it. Period-accurate 1892–1893 Saint Paul, Minnesota. Wide cinematic 16:9, single unified frame, no seam, no border. ABSOLUTE RULE: zero human faces in any image. Achieve this by showing ONLY hands, ONLY feet, ONLY objects, ONLY silhouettes backlit so dark the face is black shadow, or ONLY figures at extreme distance too small to have legible faces. Do NOT attempt over-the-shoulder or back-to-camera shots — these generate faces. Show evidence of people, not the people themselves.
scenes: A plain dinner table in a Saint Paul home in 1892 — the scene is a CLOSE-UP OF THE TABLE SURFACE ONLY: six pairs of hands and lower forearms reach in from all sides of the frame toward the dishes, bread, and pitcher at the center. The hands are of different sizes — some adult, some child-sized, some mid-age. Heads and faces are entirely ABOVE the top of the frame, cut off, not present. Only the table surface, the food, and the reaching hands from all sides. Warm lamplight from above, the wood grain and the dishes and six pairs of hands filling the frame. Sun-faded vintage tones.

A close-up of a flat iron and a white tablecloth on a kitchen table in 1892 Saint Paul — the heavy iron pressed against the cloth mid-stroke, steam rising from the fabric in a soft wisp, the cloth in smooth and rippled sections showing where it has and has not been pressed. NO PERSON visible. Only the iron, the cloth, the rising steam. A coal stove is blurred in the background. Warm amber light from a window. Sun-faded vintage tones, the domestic still-life of a young woman's morning work without the woman in it.

A long quiet Saint Paul residential street in 1892 seen from the far end: at the very far end of the block, tiny in the frame, a single female figure in a dark skirt walks away from us — so distant she is a small silhouette, no face legible, no features visible, just a small dark shape moving away down the long tree-lined street toward a distant intersection. The street fills the frame; the figure is small. Sun-faded vintage tones, the long empty street and the tiny receding shape.

A plain closed wooden door at the end of a dim hallway in a doctor's office in 1892 Saint Paul. A man's hat — a dark wide-brimmed felt hat — hangs on an iron hook on the wall beside the door. A thin line of light shows under the door crack. The hallway is empty of any person. No human figure, only the closed door, the hat on its hook, and the light beneath the door. Sun-faded vintage tones, the hat hanging beside the closed door the whole subject.

Bright spring sunlight pouring through an open bedroom window in Saint Paul in April 1893 — the window is the whole subject, the light nearly overexposed, spilling across the sill and onto the floor and the edge of a bed in the foreground. A white coverlet on the bed catches the light in a wide bright stripe. No person's face is visible — only the edge of a form under white coverlets at the bottom of the frame, cut off below the chin by the frame edge. The window and the light fill the image. Sun-faded vintage tones, the spring light mercilessly bright.

An extreme close-up of TWO HANDS ONLY — a pale young woman's hand lying open and still, and a second woman's cupped hand beside it receiving a small ruby brooch being placed from one palm into the other. Nothing above the wrists. The brooch: a small oval, dark red faceted stone in gold filigree. Warm side light on the hands and the red stone. No faces, no bodies, no background figures. Sun-faded vintage tones, the ruby the richest point of color.

A close-up of a single sheet of paper on a worn kitchen table in 1893 — folded open flat, left column of handwritten names with heavy ink X marks through them, right column of names with no marks. The writing is period cursive, the individual letters blurred and illegible. ONE large weathered hand presses a pen nib against the paper just completing an X mark — only the hand visible, nothing above the wrist. No other person in frame. Warm lamplight. Sun-faded vintage tones.

A dark kitchen in Saint Paul in 1893, lit only by one low lamp: two SILHOUETTES — a large seated figure at a kitchen table and a standing figure beside them with one hand resting on the seated figure's head. The lamp is positioned BEHIND the two figures so they are backlit into near-black silhouettes with no face detail visible — just dark shapes, the outline of broad shoulders, the outline of the standing figure's arm and hand. An empty chair sits across the table, visible in the lamplight. The silhouettes, the empty chair, the low lamp. Sun-faded vintage tones.
